---
title: "Introduction to Survival Analysis"
format:
  html: default
  revealjs:
    output-file: intro-to-survival-analysis-slides.html
  pdf:
    output-file: intro-to-survival-analysis-handout.pdf
  docx:
    output-file: intro-to-survival-analysis-handout.docx

---

{{< include shared-config.qmd >}}

# Overview

This chapter introduces **survival analysis** (also known as *time-to-event analysis*):

- Survival outcomes and censoring mechanisms
- Key distribution functions: PDF, CDF, survival function, inverse survival (quantile) function, hazard, and cumulative hazard
- Parametric models for survival data (exponential, Weibull)
- Nonparametric estimation: the Kaplan-Meier product-limit estimator
- The log-rank test for comparing survival curves
- The Nelson-Aalen cumulative hazard estimator
- The empirical inverse survival function

# Time-to-event outcomes {.smaller}

**Survival analysis** is a framework for modeling *time-to-event* outcomes. It is used in:

-   clinical trials, where the event is often death or
recurrence of disease.
-   engineering reliability analysis, where the event is
failure of a device or system.
-   insurance, particularly life insurance, where the
event is death.

:::{.callout-note}
The term *survival analysis* is a bit misleading. Survival outcomes can sometimes be analyzed using binomial models (logistic regression). *Time-to-event models* or *survival time analysis* might be a better name.
:::

# Time-to-event outcome distributions

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-vars.qmd >}}

# Distribution functions for time-to-event variables

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-dists.qmd >}}

# Parametric Models for Time-to-Event Outcomes

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-intro-parametric-models.qmd >}}

# Nonparametric Survival Analysis

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-nonparametric-surv-intro.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exm-6mp-leukemia}
#### Clinical Trial for Pediatric Acute Leukemia

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-6mp.qmd >}}

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

# The Kaplan-Meier Product Limit Estimator {#sec-km}

::: notes
Before deriving the estimator, we establish two general probability facts
its derivation relies on:
the interval failure probability $q_j(\tau)$,
and a lemma expressing a conditional survival probability as a ratio of
survival function values.
:::

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_def-interval-fail-prob.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-KM.qmd >}}

# Using the `survival` package in R

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-R.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

# The log-rank test

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-logrank-test.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

:::{#exm-bmt}
#### Bone Marrow Transplant Data

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-exm-bmt.qmd >}}

:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

# Discretized likelihood building blocks {#sec-surv-lik-building-blocks}

::: notes
The interval failure probability $q_j(\tau)$ and the conditional survival
ratio lemma were introduced earlier, ahead of @sec-km,
since the Kaplan-Meier derivation itself relies on the conditional
survival ratio.
:::

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_def-first-order-interval-fail-prob.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_lem-surv-discretized.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_lem-censored-lik.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_def-interval-lik-factor.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_lem-lik-by-interval.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

# Nelson-Aalen Estimates of Cumulative Hazard and Survival {#sec-nelson-aalen}

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-surv-nelson-aalen.qmd >}}

# Empirical Inverse Survival Function

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-emp-inv-survf.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

# Interval Censoring

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-interval-censoring.qmd >}}

# Left-Truncation

{{< include _subfiles/intro-to-survival-analysis/_sec-left-truncation.qmd >}}


# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
